title: Low-Income Individuals Use Medicaid For Affordable Dental Care
article: Medicare is a government-funded health insurance program for those aged 65 and older, as well as those with disabilities or illnesses. However, when it comes to dental services, the coverage is limited and it's quite challenging to determine which services are actually included. Let's take a closer look at what Medicare's dental coverage entails.

title: Low-Income Individuals Use Medicaid For Affordable Dental Care
article: To begin, it's important to note that Medicare doesn't cover dental procedures that are primarily aimed at improving oral health. However, it does provide coverage for other types of dental care. If dental procedures are necessary for radiation treatment of certain jaw-related diseases, Medicare can offer coverage.

title: Low-Income Individuals Use Medicaid For Affordable Dental Care
article: Furthermore, Medicare extends its coverage to dental surgery required for certain surgical procedures like heart valve replacement, facial or jaw fractures, etc. Dental splints and wiring are also included in the coverage. It is important to note, however, that Medicare does not cover postoperative dental care.

title: Low-Income Individuals Use Medicaid For Affordable Dental Care
article: Dental-related hospitalization may be covered in some situations. For example, Medicare covers hospitalization when dental procedures cause life-threatening conditions that require monitoring.

title: Low-Income Individuals Use Medicaid For Affordable Dental Care
article: In sum, Medicare's coverage for dental care has limitations. It's necessary to understand what dental services are eligible for coverage. In cases of confusion or uncertainties about insurance coverage, insurance providers can be contacted to ensure that the best possible dental care is provided.
